rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in the component type scope and implementation annexes in the implementation scope
Use subcomponent ports only when they are represented through valid variables or outputs in the component type contract
Do not use AADL property names as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
Ensure that AGREE assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports are placed in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Align helper declaration types with the expression types to avoid type mismatches
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Use `eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;` for range comparisons
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
